core_mechanism: renormalization-group scale flow of a coupling that develops a non-trivial infrared fixed point as a control parameter is tuned, with a critical threshold of that parameter separating a spontaneously-broken-symmetry phase from a scale-invariant (fixed-point) phase
brief_reason: The abstract says raising the control parameter drives the system to a non-trivial infrared fixed point (scale invariance) above a critical value while below it the symmetry is spontaneously broken, which is exactly an RG scale-flow whose fixed-point structure changes across a critical threshold in a tuning parameter.
